analysis.note: Particle; tu + ālambanam gives tv ālambanaṃ by external sandhi.
Neuter nominative singular, construed as the subject of upalabhate and the referent of sthitam.
Part of the fused printed cluster naivopalabhate: na + eva + upalabhate.
Strengthens the negation; fused in naivopalabhate.
Ātmanepada present, kartari/deponent sense: ‘perceives, apprehends’; fused in naivopalabhate.
Past passive participle used predicatively with jñānam: ‘established, standing’.

samasa.vigraha: vijñaptir eva mātram; tasya bhāvaḥ vijñaptimātratvam

analysis.note: Locative of the abstract -tva formation from vijñapti-mātra; construed with sthitam, ‘established in ...’.

verification.detail: compound stem vijñaptimātratva as Napumsaka/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Locative singular of a nested compound: ‘in the absence of an appearance of the graspable/object’.

verification.detail: compound stem grāhyābhābhāva as Pum/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Ablative of cause: ‘because of non-grasping of that’. The antecedent of tad is left as the verse’s ‘that’.

verification.detail: compound stem tadagraha as Pum/Panchami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

justifications.decision: yadā and tadā are read as a matched temporal pair, with tu as an intervening contrastive particle.
options_considered: yadā ... tadā as a temporal-correlative pair
a looser conditional ‘if ... then’ without temporal force

evidence.quote: MW s.v. yadā: ‘when, at what time, whenever (generally followed by the correlatives tadā...)’; MW s.v. tadā: ‘at that time, then ... correlative of yadā’.
evidence.translation: The grammar and lexicon support a ‘when ... then’ construction.

justifications.decision: The clause is construed as ‘cognition does not apprehend an object-support’, not as ‘it does not apprehend knowledge’ or as a compound-like phrase.
options_considered: jñānam as nominative subject and ālambanam as object
ālambanaṃ jñānaṃ as a quasi-compound phrase ‘object-supported cognition’
justifications.chosen: jñānam is the nominative subject; ālambanam is the accusative object.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. upa-√labh: ‘to perceive, behold ... understand, learn, know’; MW s.v. ālambana: ‘depending on or resting upon ... supporting’; MW s.v. jñāna: ‘knowing ... knowledge’.
evidence.translation: upalabhate is semantically transitive; ālambanam is the natural object, while jñānam is the neuter nominative subject and is resumed by sthitam.

justifications.decision: eva strengthens na rather than adding a separate assertion such as ‘indeed’.
options_considered: separate adverbial ‘indeed’ with weak connection to na
justifications.chosen: na eva is rendered as emphatic negation, ‘does not ... at all’.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. na: ‘not, no’; MW s.v. eva: ‘strengthening the idea expressed by any word ... just, exactly, only, even, alone, merely’.
evidence.translation: In the fused form naivopalabhate, eva intensifies the negative predication.

justifications.decision: The word is parsed as locative singular of vijñapti-mātra-tva, not normalized to the vulgate vijñāna-mātratve.
justifications.chosen: vijñaptimātratve means ‘in representation-only-ness’ / ‘in the state of mere vijñapti’.

evidence.quote: The supplied bhāṣya reading has vijñaptimātratve; MW s.v. vijñapti: ‘information, report ...’; MW s.v. mātra: ‘ifc. measure, quantity’; -tva forms an abstract state.
evidence.translation: Grammatically this is the state or condition of being ‘vijñapti-mātra’. In Yogācāra context I render vijñapti as ‘representation’ while retaining the Sanskrit.

justifications.decision: The bhāṣya reading is parsed as grāhya-ābhā-abhāva, a nested tatpuruṣa, rather than as simple grāhyābhāva ‘absence of the graspable’.
options_considered: grāhyasya ābhāyāḥ abhāvaḥ, ‘absence of the graspable’s appearance’
grāhyasya abhāvaḥ, ‘absence of the graspable/object’, following the vulgate-style reading
justifications.chosen: grāhyābhābhāve is ‘in the absence of the appearance of the graspable/object’.

evidence.quote: grāhya + ābhā + abhāve gives grāhyābhābhāve by internal sandhi; MW s.v. grāhya: ‘to be seized or taken’; MW s.v. ābhā: ‘splendour, light’; MW s.v. abhāva: ‘nonexistence, nullity, absence’.
evidence.translation: The extra ābhā in the supplied reading makes the absent item an ‘appearance’ of the grāhya, not simply the grāhya itself.

justifications.decision: tadagrahāt is analysed as a tatpuruṣa tasya agrahaḥ in the ablative singular, giving the reason for sthitam vijñaptimātratve.
options_considered: causal ablative ‘because of non-grasping of that’
taking tad as a separate pronoun outside the compound
justifications.chosen: tadagrahāt is a causal ablative: ‘because of non-grasping of that’.

evidence.quote: MW s.v. tad: ‘it, that’; MW s.v. agraha: ‘non-acceptance’; the ending -āt marks ablative singular.
evidence.translation: The transparent compound and ablative case support a causal construal, ‘because of the non-grasping/non-apprehension of that’.

translation:
When, however, cognition (jñāna)
does not apprehend an object-support (ālambana) at all,
then it is established in representation-only-ness (vijñaptimātratva):
for, when the graspable-object appearance is absent,
there is non-grasping of that.
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind or variant-normalizing translation could easily replace the supplied bhāṣya reading vijñaptimātratve with the vulgate vijñānamātratve and thereby shift the technical term from vijñapti to vijñāna.
It could miss the extra ābhā in grāhyābhābhāve and translate merely ‘in the absence of the object’ rather than ‘in the absence of the object-appearance’.
It could take both ālambanam and jñānam as accusatives, yielding an awkward ‘does not apprehend object-support knowledge’, rather than taking jñānam as the subject and ālambanam as the object.
analyzer_disagreements: jñānam: ByT5 gives jñāna_SANe, apparently accusative/neuter singular. I choose Prathama Eka because jñānam supplies the subject of upalabhate, while ālambanam is the accusative object; sthitam also agrees with jñānam as a predicate. If SANe was intended as nom./acc. ambiguity, this is a disambiguation rather than a substantive disagreement.
sthitam: ByT5 lemmatizes the form to √sthā. For the requested subanta analysis I give the inflected nominal stem sthita, Napumsaka Prathama Eka, while noting its derivation from √sthā.
upalabhate: ByT5 effectively treats upalabh as the lemma. Per instruction, I separate the bare root labh from the prefix upa.
vijñaptimātratve and tadagrahāt: ByT5 splits the compounds to final members tva and agraha. Per instruction, I give the whole inflected compound stems vijñaptimātratva and tadagraha.
open_questions: No commentary span was supplied in this ablation run; the exact antecedent intended by tad in tadagrahāt cannot be fixed beyond the grammatical ‘that’.
The English rendering ‘representation’ for vijñapti is contextually Yogācāra, but the supplied MW entry alone would also allow more general renderings such as ‘notification’ or ‘information’.
